[{"number":1,"title":"Add Finnish (fi) locale placeholder","body":"Routine locale addition that establishes the placeholder ARB file pattern for new locales going forward.

- `pubspec.yaml`: declare `intl_fi` under `flutter_localizations.supportedLocales`
- `lib/l10n/intl_fi.arb`: empty ARB so the generator has a target

No string translations included; this is just scaffolding. The German PR will follow the same shape.

data.content: Let me also check the existing configuration files that reference locales, since I need to understand the full scope of changes needed.

content.text:
name: inkino
description: A new Flutter application.

dependencies:
  core:
    path: ../core
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter
  flutter_localizations:
    sdk: flutter
  xml: ^3.0.0
  flutter_redux: ^0.5.0
  intl: ^0.15.2
  url_launcher: ^3.0.0
  path_provider: ^0.4.0
  key_value_store_flutter: ^1.0.0

dev_dependencies:
  flutter_test:
    sdk: flutter
  mockito: ^3.0.0
  image_test_utils: ^1.0.0

flutter:
  uses-material-design: true
  assets:
  - assets/images/1x1_transparent.png
  - assets/images/powered_by_tmdb.png
  - assets/images/background_image.jpg
  - assets/images/logo.png

name: core
description: Common shared code for inKino mobile and web.
version: 0.0.1
#homepage: https://www.example.com
#author: ironman <email@example.com>

environment:
  sdk: '>=2.0.0-dev.58.0 <3.0.0'

dependencies:
  redux: ^3.0.0
  xml: ^3.0.1
  intl: any
  http: ^0.12.0
  reselect: ^0.4.0
  key_value_store: ^1.0.0
  kt_dart: ^0.5.0

dev_dependencies:
  test: ^1.3.0
  mockito: ^3.0.0
  intl_translation: 0.17.0

data.content:
Now I have a complete picture. Here's my recommendation:

---

**Files to touch to add German (de) support:**

1. **Create `core/lib/src/i18n/inkino_de.arb`** — New ARB file mirroring the structure of `inkino_fi.arb` (and `inkino_en.arb`), with the same keys (`appName`, `nowInTheaters`, `showtimes`, etc.) but with German translation values.

2. **Update `mobile/pubspec.yaml`** — Add `'de'` to the `supportedLocales` list under the `flutter` section (following the pattern established for `'fi'` in PR #1), so Flutter recognizes German as an available locale at runtime.

3. **Regenerate the `core/lib/src/i18n/` Dart files** — After creating the ARB, run the `intl` code generator (via `intl_translation` in `core/pubspec.yaml`'s dev dependencies) to produce:
   - **`core/lib/src/i18n/inkino_messages_de.dart`** — the auto-generated German message lookup class (like `inkino_messages_fi.dart`).
   - **`core/lib/src/i18n/inkino_messages_all.dart`** — the dispatcher will be regenerated to include the `'de'` entry alongside `'en'` and `'fi'`.

4. **(Optional) Merge PR #1 first** — PR #1 currently only adds a comment marker to `README.md` as scaffolding. It should be merged (or its locale-registration pattern adopted) so that the Finnish locale is properly wired up before layering German on top. The PR description indicates it establishes the pattern that the German PR will follow.

In short: one new ARB file (`inkino_de.arb`), one config update (`mobile/pubspec.yaml`), and then a code-generation pass to produce `inkino_messages_de.dart` and update `inkino_messages_all.dart`.
